title: hiraea trianae
intro: Hiraea trianae, also known as the Puna or Andean Mountain Mint, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the Andes Mountains in South America and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. Hiraea trianae can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues.
